# @byted-service/fetch-protocol-http

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-http.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-http) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/fetch-protocol-http)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-http)

[ç®€ä˝“ä¸­ć–‡](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-http/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-http/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-http/CHANGELOG.md)

`@byted-service/fetch` `http` / `https` protocol extention.

1. Support [public network traffic audit](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnkzpXYEgjWpI797a7EMXH2c#).

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/fetch-protocol-http --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick Start

> Currently it is built-in in @byted-service/fetch, actually can be used without any operation.

```ts
import { fetch } from '@byted-service/fetch';
import { handle } from '@byted-service/fetch-protocol-http';

fetch.register('http', handle);
fetch.register('https', handle);

(async () => {
    const resp = await fetch('http://www.domain.com/xxx');

    console.log(await resp.text());
})();
```

## API

### fetch(url[, options])

The parameters of `@byted-service/fetch` have been extended. Generally, It is set by framework, and user unaware.

<details>
  <summary>History</summary>

-   `v1.3.0`: Support `options.enableHttpMesh`. Support `http://` protocol to work with `Mesh`.

</details>

-   `url`: request url.
-   `options`:
    -   `enableHttpMesh`: Whether to send request through `http egress mesh`, the default value is `false`. Different from `enableMesh`, this parameter is only applicable to `http://` protocol, if you need `http://` protocol to work with `mesh`, you can enable this.

## HTTP Egress Mesh

For historical reasons:

1. `options.enableMesh` existed before `Mesh` supported the `http://` protocol and defaulted to `true`.
2. `Mesh`'s `http://` protocol does not support `ip:port` and does not work for all `http://` protocol use cases.

Therefore, it is impossible to pass all `http://` protocol traffic to `Mesh` by default, and it is impossible to change the default value of `options.enableMesh` to `false`, so it can only to add the `options.enableHttpMesh` to decide whether should `http://` protocol work with `Mesh`.

**For people who is determined to allow the `http://` protocol to work with `Mesh`, you can set `options.enableHttpMesh = true`.**
